User.title: User
User.description: Represents a customer or administrative user of the VOID WEAR application. User authentication details are managed by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's primary email address, used for communication.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Stores a physical address, typically associated with a user or an order for shipping and billing purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this address. (Relationship: User 1:N Address)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the street address (e.g., apartment, suite number).

stateProvince.description: The state or province of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal or zip code of the address.

isDefault.description: Indicates if this is the user's default address for its type.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a core product item available for purchase in the VOID WEAR store, containing general information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

basePrice.description: The base price of the product before any variant adjustments.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs for product images.

category.description: The main category the product belongs to (e.g., 'Apparel', 'Accessories', 'Footwear').

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the product, often used in product page URLs.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the product was added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the product details were last updated.

ProductVariant.title: ProductVariant
ProductVariant.description: Represents a specific variation of a product (e.g., size, color) with its unique SKU, stock quantity, and potential price adjustment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductVariant entity.

productId.description: Reference to the parent Product. (Relationship: Product 1:N ProductVariant)

size.description: The size of the product variant (e.g., 'S', 'M', 'L', 'XL', 'One Size').

color.description: The color of the product variant (e.g., 'Black', 'Cosmic Grey', 'Vibrant White').

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for this specific product variant.

stockQuantity.description: The current number of units available in stock for this variant.

priceAdjustment.description: The monetary adjustment to the Product.basePrice for this specific variant. Can be positive or negative.

variantImageUrl.description: An optional URL for an image specific to this variant.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the product variant was added.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the product variant details were last updated.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a user's shopping cart, holding items they intend to purchase before checkout.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this cart. (Relationship: User 1:1 Cart)

status.description: The current status of the cart (e.g., 'active', 'abandoned', 'converted').

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the cart was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the cart was last updated.

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single product variant and its quantity added to a user's shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the Cart this item belongs to. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

productVariantId.description: Reference to the specific ProductVariant added to the cart. (Relationship: ProductVariant 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product variant in the cart.

addedAt.description: The timestamp when this item was added to the cart.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's completed or pending order, tracking payment, shipping, and associated details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

orderNumber.description: A human-readable, unique identifier for the order (e.g., 'VOID-12345').

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including taxes and shipping.

currency.description: The currency code for the order (e.g., 'INR', 'USD', 'EUR').

paymentStatus.description: The current status of the payment (e.g., 'pending', 'paid', 'failed', 'refunded').

shippingStatus.description: The current status of the order's shipping (e.g., 'processing', 'shipped', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address used for shipping this order. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

billingAddressId.description: Optional reference to the Address used for billing, if different from shipping. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

paymentProviderTransactionId.description: The unique transaction ID provided by the payment gateway (e.g., Razorpay).

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the order (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'UPI', 'Razorpay').

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the order was initially created in the system.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the order details were last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific product variant and its quantity included in a customer's order at the time of purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productVariantId.description: Reference to the specific ProductVariant purchased. (Relationship: ProductVariant 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product variant purchased in this order item.

priceAtOrder.description: The individual price of this product variant at the time the order was placed (including any variant adjustments, excluding quantity).

itemName.description: A snapshot of the product name at the time of order for historical accuracy.

itemSku.description: A snapshot of the product variant SKU at the time of order for historical accuracy.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the user themselves, identified by `userId` matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores shipping/billing addresses for a specific user. Includes implicit `userId` from path and explicit `userId` in document for strong authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique identifier for the address document.

definition.description: Stores public product information. Read access is public for all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product document.

definition.description: Stores public product variant details (size, color, SKU, stock, price adjustment). Read access is public for all users. Write access is restricted to administrators. Includes explicit `productId` in document for strong authorization independence.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent product.

params.name: variantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product variant document.

definition.description: Stores the single active shopping cart for a specific user. The document ID 'active_cart' is used as a convention for the current cart. Includes implicit `userId` from path and explicit `userId` in document for strong authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this cart.

definition.description: Stores individual items for a user's active shopping cart. Includes implicit `userId` and `cartId` (from the 'active_cart' document) from path, and explicit `cartId` in document for strong authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent cart.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the cart item document.

definition.description: Stores historical and pending orders for a specific user. Includes implicit `userId` from path and explicit `userId` in document for strong authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order document.

definition.description: Stores the specific items purchased within a user's order. Includes implicit `userId` and `orderId` from path, as well as explicit `orderId` in document for strong authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order item document.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC). Contains documents representing users with 'admin' privileges. The existence of a document here (where `adminId` matches `request.auth.uid`) grants administrative access, used for simple `exists()` checks in security rules.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of the user who holds the admin role.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for VOID WEAR is designed around core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
Authorization context is denormalized to avoid `get()` calls in security rules, which can break atomic operations and complicate debugging. For all user-owned data (e.g., Addresses, Carts, Orders, and their items), the `userId` is inherently part of the document's path (`/users/{userId}/...`). Additionally, critical parent IDs (`userId`, `cartId`, `orderId`) are explicitly stored as fields within the respective child documents (`Address`, `Cart`, `CartItem`, `Order`, `OrderItem`). For instance, an `OrderItem` document at `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}/items/{itemId}` will contain both `userId` and `orderId` fields. This design makes authorization checks self-contained within each document, allowing rules to evaluate permissions solely based on `request.auth.uid` and the document's own fields, thus enabling robust atomic operations and simplifying rule logic.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) through Structural Segregation:**
To support Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) and prevent security rules from acting as data filters (which are inefficient and insecure), collections are segregated based on their security posture:

1.  **Public Catalog Data:** The `/products` and `/products/{productId}/variants` collections are designed for public read access. All documents within these collections share the same security requirement: anyone can read them. This ensures that `list` queries on these collections will always return a complete, authorized result set without needing complex rule-based filtering.
2.  **Private User-Owned Data:** Collections under `/users/{userId}/...` (e.g., `/users/{userId}/addresses`, `/users/{userId}/carts/active_cart/items`, `/users/{userId}/orders`) are strictly private to the authenticated user. Security rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid == userId` (where `userId` is obtained from the path and denormalized fields). This structural segregation guarantees that `list` operations within these user-specific paths will only retrieve data belonging to the requesting user, satisfying QAP principles.
3.  **Administrative Roles (DBAC):** The `/app_roles/admin/{adminId}` collection is used for Database-Backed Access Control. The mere existence of a document with an `adminId` matching `request.auth.uid` grants administrative privileges. This provides a clear, debuggable mechanism for elevated access that does not rely on custom claims.

**Access Modeling and Data Clarity:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** Consistently applied for user data (e.g., `/users/{userId}/addresses`).
*   **Hierarchical Paths:** Used for nested data (e.g., `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}/items/{itemId}`).
*   **Explicit State:** Entities like `Cart` and `Order` use clear `status` fields for predictable state management. Predictable schemas with explicit field names (e.g., `ownerId`, `createdAt`) are maintained throughout.
